```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parâmetros aleatórios
lambda <- sample(c(2, 3, 4, 5, 6), 1)
n      <- sample(c(25, 36, 50, 100), 1)
delta  <- sample(-5:5, 1)
sum_x  <- n * lambda + delta

## Resposta correta
correct <- round(sum_x / n, 4)

## Alternativas incorretas plausíveis
w1 <- round(sum_x / (n - 1), 4)
w2 <- round(log(sum_x / n), 4)
w3 <- round(exp(sum_x / n), 4)
w4 <- round(sum_x / n + 0.5, 4)

## Garantir unicidade
if (w1 == correct) w1 <- round(sum_x / (n - 2), 4)
if (w2 == correct) w2 <- round(log(sum_x / n + 0.01), 4)
if (w3 == correct) w3 <- round(exp(sum_x / n) + 0.1, 4)
if (w4 == correct) w4 <- round(sum_x / n + 1, 4)
if (w2 == w1)      w2 <- round(log(sum_x / n) + 0.1, 4)
if (w3 == w1 || w3 == w2) w3 <- round(sum_x / n - 0.5, 4)
if (w4 == w1 || w4 == w2 || w4 == w3) w4 <- round(sum_x / n + 0.25, 4)

## Embaralhamento SEMPRE
all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========

Seja $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} \text{Poisson}(\lambda)$. A log-verossimilhança é $\ell(\lambda) = \left(\sum_{i=1}^n X_i\right)\log\lambda - n\lambda + \text{const}$. Derivando e igualando a zero obtém-se $\hat{\lambda}_{MV} = \bar{X}$. Com $n = `r n`$ e $\sum_{i=1}^n X_i = `r sum_x`$, calcule $\hat{\lambda}_{MV}$.

Answerlist
----------

* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========

A equação de verossimilhança é:
$$\frac{d\ell}{d\lambda} = \frac{\sum X_i}{\lambda} - n = 0 \implies \hat{\lambda}_{MV} = \frac{\sum X_i}{n} = \frac{`r sum_x`}{`r n`} = `r correct`.$$
A segunda derivada $d^2\ell/d\lambda^2 = -\sum X_i / \lambda^2 < 0$ confirma que é um máximo.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap3S2Q3_emv_poisson
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
